content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
February 26,
1993
Honorable Anthony A.
Anderson
Judge,
Shasta County Municipal Court
1500 Court Street
Redding,
CA
96001
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A—93-081
Dear Judge Anderson:
You have requested advice concerning your filing obligations
under. the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the
“Act”) •1/
QUESTION
Were you required to file a semiannual campaign disclosure
statement for calendar year 1992?
CONCLUSION
If you met the requirements of Section 84200(3) (A) (B)
you
were not required to file a semiannual campaign statement for
1992.
FACTS
You are a judge for the Shasta County Municipal Court.
In
1992 your name was not on the ballot.
In addition,
during 1992
you did not receive any contributions or make any contributions in
excess of $1,000.
1’
Government Code Sections
81000—91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95812 •
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
A-93-081
Fg?
2
ANALYSIS
The Act requires candidates and officeholders to file
periodic campaign statements.
(Sections 84200-84226.)
However,
the Act also provides that under certain conditions judges are not
required to file semiannual campaign statements.
Section 84200
(3) (A) (B)
states:
(3)
A judge who is not listed on the ballot for
reelection to,
or recall from,
any elective office during a
calendar year shall not file semiannual statements pursuant
to this subdivision for any six-month period in that year if
both of the following apply:
(A)
The judge has not received any contributions.
(B)
The only expenditures made by the judge during the
calendar year are contributions from the judge’s personal
funds to other candidates or committees totaling less than
one thousand dollars
($1,000).
Please do not hesitate to contact me at
(916)
322—5662 if
you have additional questions concerning this matter.
Sincerely,
Jeff Marschner
General Counsel
By:
Lynda Cassady
Political Reform Consultant

QUESTION: Were you required to file a semiannual campaign disclosure
statement for calendar year 1992?

CONCLUSION: If you met the requirements of Section 84200(3) (A) (B)
you
were not required to file a semiannual campaign statement for
1992.
